# Vygl

> Vygl is an AI-powered security scanning platform that finds vulnerabilities across code, dependencies, secrets, infrastructure, and container images. It combines multiple scan engines (SAST, SCA, Secrets, IaC, Container) with AI-powered triage to help development teams cut through scanner noise and focus on real issues.

## What Vygl Does

Vygl scans your codebase and container images using five specialized engines and then uses AI to triage the results:

- **SAST (Static Application Security Testing)** via OpenGrep: Detects SQL injection, XSS, command injection, path traversal, SSRF, and insecure deserialization in source code.
- **SCA (Software Composition Analysis)** via OSV: Identifies vulnerable dependencies like Log4Shell and Spring4Shell, with CVE tracking across 7+ programming languages and CycloneDX SBOM export.
- **Secrets Detection** via Gitleaks: Finds hardcoded API keys, database credentials, JWT secrets, and private keys using 600+ detection patterns.
- **IaC (Infrastructure as Code) Scanning** via Checkov: Catches public S3 buckets, unrestricted security groups, overprivileged IAM roles, and container misconfigurations in Terraform, Kubernetes, Docker, and CloudFormation.
- **Container Scanning**: Scans container images from the CLI or via registry integration. Finds vulnerable OS and application packages across every layer, and correlates container CVEs with SCA findings in the source code.
- **AI-Powered Triage**: Every finding is reviewed by an LLM that scores confidence, identifies true vs false positives, and suggests fixes. Bring your own LLM provider or use Vygl's.

## Key Features

- AI Security Brief: One-click AI-generated security report analyzing all findings with executive summary, priority actions, and quick wins.
- Per-Image AI Analysis: Dedicated AI-generated security brief for each container image, with base image upgrade advice and priority package bumps.
- CVE Watch: Continuous monitoring of every package in your SBOM against new vulnerability disclosures, with instant Slack, email, and webhook alerts when a new CVE matches a package you ship.
- Source ↔ Container Correlation: Match SCA findings in source code with the same CVEs in built images for full supply-chain visibility.
- SHA-256 fingerprinting for smart deduplication across scans.
- Policy as Code with monitor, block, and disable modes.
- Privacy-first: scans run in your environment; only findings metadata reaches the cloud.
- Runs as a Docker container in CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or any Docker-compatible pipeline), or as a fully-managed scan via Git provider integration.
- Cloud dashboard for triage, tracking, and managing findings across projects, branches, and container images.

## Integrations

- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI IDE integration (available) — query source and container findings, verify issues, correlate CVEs, inspect images, and check project health from Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible IDE
- Managed Scans via GitHub App, GitLab OAuth, and Bitbucket OAuth (available) — zero CI/CD config, auto-clone on push/PR
- Container Registry: Harbor (available); ECR, Google Artifact Registry, and Docker Hub (coming soon)
- GitHub PR comments (available)
- GitLab MR comments (available)
- Slack notifications (available)
- Microsoft Teams (coming soon)
- Custom Webhooks (coming soon)

## Pricing

- Free for open-source projects.
- Invitation-only for commercial teams.

## How to Get Access

Vygl is currently invitation-only. Request access by emailing access@vygl.io.

## Links

- Website: https://vygl.io
- Contact: access@vygl.io
